archived_trends.name: Dubai as a Competitive Tax Jurisdiction
archived_trends.summary: Dubai offers a 9% corporate tax rate, with 0% rates in Free Zones, and allows for 100% foreign ownership, positioning itself as a business-friendly hub.
archived_trends.evidence: Dubai has a 9% corporate tax, with 0% rate in Free Zones.

archived_trends.name: Austria's Income Tax Reduction
archived_trends.summary: Austria is planning to reduce its top income tax rate from 55% to 50% in 2026, potentially impacting labor markets and investment.
archived_trends.evidence: Austria is planning to eliminate its highest tax bracket in 2026.

archived_trends.name: Corporate Tax Rate Adjustments - Austria
archived_trends.summary: Austria is planning to reduce its top income tax rate from 55% to 50% in 2026.
archived_trends.evidence: Austria is planning to eliminate its highest tax bracket in 2026.

archived_trends.name: US Corporate Tax Implications of Global Minimum Tax
archived_trends.summary: The US is positioned to collect additional tax revenue from its companies if they pay less than 15% in other jurisdictions, potentially impacting the competitiveness of US-based MNEs. The side-by-side arrangement exempts US companies from full Pillar Two compliance.
archived_trends.evidence: The plan would effectively allow the US to tax the difference between the 15% minimum rate and lower rates paid in countries like Ireland. US side-by-side arrangement exempts US companies from full Pillar Two compliance.

archived_trends.name: Legal Challenges to US Tariffs
archived_trends.summary: The legality of tariffs imposed under the International Emergency Economic Powers Act (IEEPA) is being challenged in court, creating uncertainty for businesses and trade policy. The Supreme Court is expected to rule in the coming months.
archived_trends.evidence: Court for International Trade (CIT) ruled IEEPA tariffs unlawful. Supreme Court expected to rule in coming months.

archived_trends.name: Corporate Tax Rate Adjustments - Portugal
archived_trends.summary: Portugal is planning to decrease its corporate income tax rate by one percentage point per year from 2026 to 2028.
archived_trends.evidence: Corporate income tax will decrease by one percentage point per year, starting from January 2026 and ending in 2028.
